project_summary: OwlNet Technologies Q2 Sprint Tasks - Avian Cortex-to-Silicon Division

tasks.title: Schedule OT-alpha ablation study working session
tasks.description: Coordinate and schedule a 90-minute working session to determine whether OT-alpha structures should be modeled as separate attention heads in the transformer or integrated into existing spatial embedding layers

tasks.title: Investigate INT8 quantisation for edge inference
tasks.description: Explore INT8 quantisation techniques to reduce inference latency below 20ms target for edge deployment branch of OwlNet v0.4

tasks.title: Adjust Cohort C electrode schedule
tasks.description: Modify electrode implant schedule to comply with reduced 60-day maximum duration per IACUC recommendation, affecting 3 subjects

tasks.title: Prepare EU AI Act compliance memo
tasks.description: Create compliance documentation for EU AI Act (Article 6, Annex III) applicability to OwlNet commercialisation for autonomous systems

tasks.title: Follow up on edge hardware chip supply delay
tasks.description: Contact chip suppliers to resolve supply chain issues blocking real-time inference on edge hardware

tasks.title: Confirm Nature Machine Intelligence submission slot
tasks.description: Secure publication slot for external peer review of OwlNet research in Nature Machine Intelligence journal

tasks.title: Update investor deck with v0.4 benchmark results
tasks.description: Incorporate latest OwlNet v0.4 training pipeline benchmarks into investor presentation materials for Series B funding

tasks.title: Complete Cohort B cortical mapping
tasks.description: Finish mapping optic tectum and inferior colliculus connectivity in 12 barn owl subjects, including analysis of novel OT-alpha, OT-beta, and OT-gamma microcolumn structures

tasks.title: Develop OwlNet v0.5 with OT-alpha attention heads
tasks.description: Implement findings from cortical mapping into next version of OwlNet, incorporating OT-alpha structures as attention mechanisms
